title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. webbiana
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. webbiana, also known as Webb's kidney vetch, is a subspecies of the kidney vetch plant that belongs to the legume family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and urinary tract issues. The health benefits of Webb's kidney vetch include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections. In herbal preparations, Webb's kidney vetch is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in topical creams and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
